rationale: The citing paper explicitly centers on estimating heterogeneous time preferences and argues prior work’s fixed/exponential discounting assumptions are limiting, while the cited paper’s core contribution is field identification of discount factors and consequences of discount-rate misspecification. That makes the cited work a plausible methodological/foundational source rather than an incidental mention. Even though the matched evidence is only a DOI record (no local citation context), this pair is strong enough to keep for full relation judgment.
